summary.note: Manually curated red flag questions grounded in knowledge base documents

questions.question: Which shoulder presentation requires same-day emergency referral?
Which finding following traumatic shoulder injury requires urgent referral to a specialist shoulder clinic?

options.c: Acute rotator cuff tear as a result of a traumatic event

questions.text_answer: Acute rotator cuff tear as a result of a traumatic event

questions.question: Which shoulder red flag requires referral following the urgent two-week cancer pathway?

options.e: Frozen shoulder with loss of rotation

questions.question: Which combination of findings following trauma or epileptic fit is a red flag for unreduced dislocation?

options.b: Loss of rotation and abnormal shoulder shape

questions.text_answer: Loss of rotation and abnormal shoulder shape

questions.question: Which clinical presentation warrants urgent secondary care referral in shoulder pain assessment?

options.a: Chronic rotator cuff tendinopathy unresponsive to physiotherapy
options.b: Red skin, fever, or a systemically unwell patient
options.d: Painful arc with positive Hawkins-Kennedy test

questions.text_answer: Red skin, fever, or a systemically unwell patient

questions.question: Which feature in a shoulder pain patient's history most strongly suggests a serious underlying pathology requiring urgent investigation?

options.b: Night pain relieved by positional change
options.d: Gradual onset of stiffness over months
options.e: Pain radiating to the deltoid region

questions.question: In patients with atraumatic shoulder instability, which finding indicates early referral to a tertiary centre is appropriate?

options.a: Mild apprehension on abduction and external rotation
options.b: Frequent attendances at A&E for shoulder relocation
options.c: Positive sulcus sign on clinical examination

questions.text_answer: Frequent attendances at A&E for shoulder relocation

questions.question: Which visceral cause of referred shoulder pain should be considered when a patient presents with acute shoulder pain and no musculoskeletal cause is identified?

options.c: Irritation of the diaphragm or phrenic nerve

questions.text_answer: Irritation of the diaphragm or phrenic nerve

questions.question: Which finding during shoulder examination should prompt immediate radiography due to suspicion of metastatic disease?

options.a: Painful arc between 60-120 degrees of abduction
options.c: Local tenderness, painful arc, and very weak resisted abduction with localized warmth
options.d: Positive Hawkins-Kennedy and Neer impingement signs
options.e: Loss of passive external rotation with hard end feel

questions.text_answer: Local tenderness, painful arc, and very weak resisted abduction with localized warmth

questions.question: Which symptom pattern in a shoulder pain patient most strongly suggests a Pancoast tumour rather than a musculoskeletal disorder?

options.a: Gradual onset of stiffness with capsular pattern
options.b: Painful arc with positive impingement signs
options.c: Rapidly increasing pain radiating into the arm with deteriorating general health
options.d: Rotator cuff weakness with subacromial tenderness
options.e: Acromioclavicular joint pain with cross-arm adduction

questions.text_answer: Rapidly increasing pain radiating into the arm with deteriorating general health
